project_description: Complete overhaul of ForgeOS — an AI-powered web app builder with a cockpit UI, workspace runner, and GitHub-to-Render deploy chain — replacing a broken multi-agent pipeline with a single Claude-powered builder, persistent memory brain, and clean agent boundaries.

decisions: Canvas built directly by Claude, not via Forge pipeline — build was too complex (10+ files, 4 functional domains) for multi-pass at current pipeline maturity. CANVAS_BUILD_REPORT.md documents the decision framework for future reference.
Single server.js architecture for Canvas — all HTML rendered server-side as template strings, no frontend build step. Makes every future Forge iteration a single-file BUILD: suggestion.
CANVAS_DATABASE_URL not NEON_DATABASE_URL — NEON_DATABASE_URL is reserved and cannot be overridden per published service. Apps with PII need dedicated DB env vars.

Proxy redirect:manual — Node fetch() follows redirects automatically by default. Proxies must always use redirect:manual or they silently follow 302s and return the final destination as 200, breaking browser URL bar and navigation.

Proxy form POST body must use URLSearchParams not JSON.stringify — Express parses urlencoded bodies into objects; re-serializing as JSON changes content-type and byte length, breaking the receiving server's body parser.
content-length must be deleted before proxying re-encoded bodies — the original content-length from the browser no longer matches after re-encoding; Node recalculates correctly when omitted.

Published apps must use fetch() with Content-Type application/json for all form submissions through the ForgeOS proxy — never standard HTML form POSTs. The proxy reliably handles JSON; urlencoded body re-encoding through the proxy has proven unreliable across multiple fix attempts.

Admin auth through ForgeOS proxy requires bearer token approach — cookie-session Set-Cookie headers don't survive the proxy reliably, and window.location.href page loads don't carry Authorization headers. Solution: issue token on login, pass as query param on first load (/admin?token=xxx), store in localStorage for subsequent requests.

GDPR/ePrivacy consent requirement: storing a persistent visitor ID in localStorage for analytics on a public-facing site with EU visitors requires user consent. The trigger is specifically the persistent identifier (_fvid) — sessionStorage IDs, vitals, errors, and page_load events do not require consent as they don't persist across sessions or identify individuals.

Analytics consent gate pattern: on public-facing sites, gate all localStorage writes (specifically _fvid) behind a consent check (localStorage.getItem('_fa_consent') === '1'). Session events, vitals, errors, and page_load can fire freely. On consent: set localStorage.setItem('_fa_consent', '1') and allow _fvid to be written.

Consent banner implementation pattern: fixed bottom-of-page banner (not a modal, not a blocker), single OK button, brief copy linking to a /privacy page. On load: if _fa_consent already set, skip banner entirely. Do not show banner on the /privacy page itself.

Privacy policy page (/privacy route) required for any public-facing ForgeOS app using analytics. Must disclose: what is collected (pageviews, clicks, scroll depth, performance metrics, JS errors), that data is stored in a first-party database and never shared with third parties, how to opt out (clear localStorage), and a contact method.

First-party analytics without third-party data sharing (no Google, no ad networks) is a significantly more favorable legal position under GDPR and CCPA. CCPA does not require a consent banner — only a privacy policy disclosing collection. EU ePrivacy requires consent specifically for persistent identifiers in localStorage.

Forge has full context to build GDPR-compliant consent banners and privacy pages for public-facing apps — include the above requirements in the build prompt. Key check: verify _fvid localStorage write is gated behind consent, banner is suppressed on the /privacy page, and privacy policy explicitly covers opt-out via localStorage clear.

Large prompt plan gate: prompts >1500 chars are blocked from direct build at server level (POST /iterate returns 400 requiresPlan: true) and client level (startProject auto-routes through plan gate). approvedPlan bypasses the block. isSuggestion bypasses the block.

startProject large prompt path: creates project with skipBuild: true, then calls /plan, shows plan approval gate — identical UX to iterateProject plan gate. Project exists in DB before any build fires.
POST /api/projects accepts skipBuild: true — creates project record without firing builder. Returns { id, runId: null, name }. Used exclusively by the large prompt plan gate path.

Context overflow pattern: position >10000 JSON parse error in build output means the builder's total token input exceeded Claude's handling capacity. Root cause is server/pipeline/model-router.js (schema in system prompt) or server/builder.js (memory/assets context not capped). Chat Agent should fire FORGE: on this pattern.

schema instruction in model-router.js is now injected as the final user message, not appended to system prompt — this frees system prompt budget on large builds and is more efficient for Claude to process.

AI Receptionist pattern: Twilio Voice → POST /incoming-call (TwiML Gather) → POST /respond (Claude conversation loop) → POST /call-complete (Resend email + HubSpot contact). Per-call state in Map keyed by CallSid. Voice must be 'alice' (built-in) not Polly.Joanna (paid add-on).
Twilio statusCallback for call-complete must be configured in Twilio console under 'Call status changes' — not inside a <Gather> verb where it is ignored.

Canvas app architecture: Node/Express backend, plain HTML/JS frontend (Mode A), fal.ai Flux Pro for background generation, DOM-overlay stickers (not canvas-drawn) for drag UX, html2canvas composite at save time, sharp upscale to 3000x3000 for heat transfer print quality, QR code via qrcode npm package linking to /download/:sessionId.

Multi-pass build pipeline: when planner detects >6 files total (filesToCreate + filesToModify), it outputs multiPass: true with a passes array. Each pass has 3-6 files max. buildWorkspaceMultiPass executes passes sequentially, each feeding accumulated files as existingFiles to the next pass.

buildWorkspaceMultiPass in server/builder.js: iterates passes, calls buildWorkspace per pass with scoped constraint block, merges output files across passes (new files added, existing updated), stores passProgress on run.stages.builder for Plan tab visibility.
planToConstraintBlock now accepts an optional passOverride parameter — when provided, generates a pass-scoped constraint block with only that pass's files permitted. Single-pass behavior unchanged.

Multi-pass plan shape: { taskSummary, approach, multiPass: true, passes: [{ passNumber, description, filesToCreate, filesToModify }] }. Single-pass shape unchanged: { taskSummary, approach, filesToCreate, filesToModify, filesOffLimits }.

Chat action buttons (.chat-build-btn, .chat-plan-btn, .chat-forge-btn) are full-width block elements with 44px min-height — required for mobile touch targets. All three were previously either unstyled or too small.

Forge correctly diagnosed the multi-pass architecture via FORGE: signal and proposed the correct implementation — the autonomy loop worked as designed. Direct implementation was only needed because building multi-pass with a broken single-pass pipeline is inherently circular.

Multi-pass builder pass prompt must be scoped: use approvedPlan.taskSummary + pass.description + first 600 chars of original prompt only. Never pass the full original prompt to each pass — it defeats the purpose of multi-pass and causes context overflow.

Multi-pass accumulated files must be filtered per pass: only send files that the current pass directly creates, modifies, or references by name. Sending all accumulated files to every pass grows context unboundedly.
Memory context hard cap in multi-pass: 600 chars max per pass (vs 2000 chars for single-pass large builds). The regular cap is too large when multiplied across passes with file context.

Forge repair apply takes 30-60 seconds: builder call + file write + git commit + push. The UI must show an explicit loading state during this time. Success shows files written and Render redeploy note. Failure shows the actual error message.

postBuildAnalysis(projectId, run) in server/chat/manager.js fires automatically after every failed build. Classifies failure via KNOWN_FAILURE_PATTERNS, saves instant diagnosis as assistant message. Pattern-matched failures need no Claude call. Unrecognized failures invoke Haiku with tight diagnostic context.

KNOWN_FAILURE_PATTERNS: 6 patterns — context_overflow (JSON position >4 digits → FORGE:), missing_api_key (401/auth → plain), json_schema_validation (Zod → FORGE:), start_failed (workspace crash → BUILD:), install_failed (npm → plain), build_failed_generic (catch-all). Add new patterns here as failure modes are discovered.

Large prompt threshold is 3000 chars (both server/index.js and client/src/App.tsx). Matches planner truncation threshold. Prompts under 3000 go direct to build; 3000+ route through plan gate. Previously 1500 which was too conservative.

Chat Agent proactive posture: on a failed build, first response must be a diagnosis. Never ask for information already in context (diagnostics, iteration history, source files, logs). [AUTO-ANALYSIS] prefixed messages are automated diagnoses — build on them, do not repeat them.

server/agents/manager.js: four focused sub-agents — file_analyst (multi-file relationship mapping), failure_analyst (deep build failure diagnosis → CAUSE/FILE/FIX), fix_verifier (pre-build fix consistency check → VALID/ISSUES/VERDICT), spec_analyst (complex prompt decomposition → JSON with features/buildOrder/fileGroups/riskAreas/estimatedPasses).

invoke_agent tool in Chat Agent: optional tool for tasks exceeding single-context analysis. Use file_analyst for 3+ file relationship questions, failure_analyst for ambiguous failures, fix_verifier for Canvas-scale multi-file changes, spec_analyst for large build prompts. Never use for simple fixes, routine diagnostics, or when the answer is already in context.

Sub-agents use Haiku at 0.1 temperature — fast, cheap, analytical. Each is a single Claude API call with a tight system prompt. No state between calls. runSubAgent(agentType, context, question) is the core dispatcher.

NEON_DATABASE_URL is a reserved system variable in ForgeOS published services and cannot be overridden per-project. Apps that need their own isolated database (especially those handling PII) must use a custom env var name like CANVAS_DATABASE_URL or APP_DATABASE_URL.

Node fetch() follows redirects automatically by default. The ForgeOS subdomain proxy must always use redirect:'manual' in fetch options, otherwise it silently follows 302s from published apps and returns the final destination page as 200 to the browser. This breaks form submissions, auth flows, and any redirect-based navigation.

Proxy form POST bodies must be re-encoded with URLSearchParams for application/x-www-form-urlencoded requests. JSON.stringify(req.body) produces the wrong content-type and wrong byte length. The content-length header must also be deleted before forwarding so Node recalculates from the actual re-encoded body.

Canvas app architecture: single server.js with all HTML as template strings, no frontend build step, all assets stored as base64 in Neon. This makes every future iteration a single-file BUILD: suggestion in Forge. Apps with this pattern are maintainable by Forge without multi-pass.

CANVAS_BUILD_REPORT.md on apps/forge-canvas branch documents: architecture decisions, full route table, schema, dependency rationale, and decision framework (single-file vs multi-pass vs single-pass) for Forge to learn from.

Published ForgeOS apps must use fetch() with JSON bodies for all form submissions — never standard HTML form action/method POSTs. The subdomain proxy reliably forwards JSON; urlencoded form data re-encoding through the proxy has multiple failure modes (content-length mismatch, Cloudflare caching, encoding differences).

Admin auth pattern for ForgeOS published apps: (1) login returns JSON { redirect, token }, (2) client stores token in localStorage, (3) redirect to protected route with ?token=xxx query param, (4) server validates token and serves page, (5) subsequent requests inject token via fetch override. This pattern works reliably through the ForgeOS subdomain proxy.

decisions_made.decision: Replace 11-prompt multi-agent pipeline with single Claude workspace builder prompt
decisions_made.rationale: Pipeline had too many inter-agent communication failure points. Single model with full context reasons through the whole build in one pass.

decisions_made.decision: Assets are global scope — no project_id
decisions_made.rationale: Project-scoped assets fell out on reforge. Global assets persist across all projects and rebuilds.

decisions_made.decision: Stage 2 tech stack unbans: React/Vue via Vite
decisions_made.rationale: CDN-based React already worked. npm-based React via Vite opens full component ecosystem with controlled bundler choice.

decisions_made.decision: Mode A / Mode B runtime structure in builder prompt
decisions_made.rationale: Without explicit modes the builder would guess when to use Vite. Mode A is default, Mode B is opt-in — clear decision tree prevents unnecessary framework use.

decisions_made.decision: All Anthropic API calls use claude-opus-4-5
decisions_made.rationale: Most capable model, chosen for quality over cost given the stakes of first-pass build quality.

decisions_made.decision: Memory extraction uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 not opus
decisions_made.rationale: Memory extraction is a lightweight classification task — haiku is fast and cheap.

decisions_made.decision: ForgeOS Brain stores memories in Neon Postgres, not a separate vector DB
decisions_made.rationale: Neon supports pgvector natively — no additional infrastructure needed.

decisions_made.decision: Replit sunset — VS Code is sole dev environment
decisions_made.rationale: Replit caused workflow friction. VS Code + GitHub + Render is cleaner and more reliable.

decisions_made.decision: Visual editor ships Phase 2 → Phase 1 → Phase 3
decisions_made.rationale: Phase 2 (direct file edit) delivers immediate value with zero pipeline risk. Phase 1 (inspect) adds element targeting. Phase 3 (bridge) closes the loop — each phase is useful standalone.

decisions_made.decision: Inspector script injected dormant into all preview HTML, activated via postMessage
decisions_made.rationale: No server round-trip needed to toggle inspect mode. Keeps the iframe self-contained and the activation instantaneous.

decisions_made.decision: All Render services upgraded to Standard tier — Starter retired
decisions_made.rationale: Always-on requirement. Starter tier spins down after inactivity.

decisions_made.decision: GitHub PAT stored in context pack only, never in chat
decisions_made.rationale: Chat history is less controlled. Context pack is attached intentionally per session.

decisions_made.decision: Safe failure over destructive success for surgical builds
decisions_made.rationale: If suggestionToConstraintBlock cannot parse the target file, all files go off-limits. A build that does nothing is recoverable in seconds; a build that rewrites wrong files can take an hour to unwind.

decisions_made.decision: Plan gate bypassed for Chat Agent build suggestions (isSuggestion: true)
decisions_made.rationale: Chat Agent already scopes the change to one file and one fix. Running the planner on top of that is redundant and causes the planner to treat the suggestion as a feature request, generating overly broad multi-file plans.

decisions_made.decision: deleteIteration auto-rolls currentRunId to previous iteration
decisions_made.rationale: Manual DB correction after deletion is error-prone. Rolling back automatically keeps the project state consistent without extra steps.

decisions_made.decision: restoreIteration uses stopApp(currentRunId) not stopAllApps()
decisions_made.rationale: stopAllApps() is a global shutdown tool — calling it during a single-project restore destroys every other running workspace. Scoped stopApp() is the correct primitive for project-level operations.

decisions_made.decision: restoreIteration fails fast on missing snapshot before any filesystem changes
decisions_made.rationale: If the snapshot doesn't exist, there's nothing to restore. Any filesystem changes before the guard would leave the workspace in a destroyed state with no valid code to run.

decisions_made.decision: loadAndRegisterRun() added to runner.js to put past snapshots back in the runs Map
decisions_made.rationale: The UI polls the runs Map directly for status and file data. A workspace that's running but not in the Map is invisible to the user — they see a spinner forever.

decisions_made.decision: HubSpot integration uses Global Secrets Vault token, not hardcoded credentials
decisions_made.rationale: Vault token is auto-injected as HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN at runtime into all generated apps — no code change needed when token rotates.

decisions_made.decision: PLAN: signal added to Chat Agent — routes multi-step fixes through plan gate instead of surgical build
decisions_made.rationale: The one-fix surgical model was the wrong tool for API migrations and deprecated endpoint rewrites. PLAN: gives the Chat Agent a second output path that matches the complexity of the fix to the right build mechanism.

decisions_made.decision: planSuggestion exempt from banned-pattern validation
decisions_made.rationale: PLAN: text must describe full migration scope — it inherently uses language like 'both routes', 'and package.json', file lists. Applying the bug-report two-sentence rule to planning text would make the plan description useless.

decisions_made.decision: 10-turn conversation window with project history summarization
decisions_made.rationale: Full conversation history at 50+ turns eats builder context budget. 10 turns gives precise recent context; overflow is summarized not discarded, so no knowledge is permanently lost.

decisions_made.decision: Streaming builder responses closed — not implemented
decisions_made.rationale: Streaming doesn't change time-to-working-app. The build pipeline starts after full JSON parse regardless. Spinner duration identical. JSON chunk parsing complexity not justified.

decisions_made.decision: extractFailureMemory replaces recordMistake at all build failure paths
decisions_made.rationale: A Haiku-generated structured lesson with category is more useful to Brain than a raw concatenated error string. Cost is negligible.

decisions_made.decision: Analytics tracker injected at publish time, not preview time
decisions_made.rationale: Published-only keeps analytics signal clean. Preview sessions are dev noise. Any republish of an existing project automatically activates the tracker with no per-project setup.

decisions_made.decision: Tracker projectId baked at publish time as slug
decisions_made.rationale: No runtime lookup needed. Slug rename triggers republish which rebakes the correct ID. Clean data separation between slug versions.

decisions_made.decision: Two-layer large prompt enforcement — server blocks at /iterate, client auto-routes in startProject
decisions_made.rationale: Single-layer enforcement can be bypassed (e.g. direct API call). Both layers must agree: server returns 400 requiresPlan, client never sends large prompts to /iterate without approvedPlan.

decisions_made.decision: Schema instruction moved from system prompt to final user message in model-router.js
decisions_made.rationale: System prompt is already large on complex builds. Appending the full JSON schema to it compounded context usage and caused overflow. Claude handles schema instruction in user turn more efficiently.

decisions_made.decision: Forge should self-repair context overflow — Chat Agent has FORGE: trigger for this pattern
decisions_made.rationale: Manually patching ForgeOS instead of letting Forge diagnose and fix itself undermines the self-repair pipeline. Context overflow is now a named failure mode in the infrastructure map.

decisions_made.decision: Multi-pass threshold is >6 total files
decisions_made.rationale: A single Claude builder call can safely handle ~6 files without hitting context limits. Beyond that, sequential passes are required to keep each call within budget.

decisions_made.decision: buildWorkspaceMultiPass is internal to builder.js — runner.js unchanged
decisions_made.rationale: The multi-pass orchestration is a builder concern. Keeping it inside builder.js means the runner, the API routes, and the client all work without modification. The run object's existing structure carries all needed state.

decisions_made.decision: Files accumulate across passes with merge semantics
decisions_made.rationale: Later passes need to import from earlier ones (e.g. routes referencing server.js). Each pass receiving the full accumulated file set ensures the builder can write correct import paths and references.

decisions_made.decision: Pattern matching before Claude call in postBuildAnalysis
decisions_made.rationale: Known failure patterns (context overflow, missing API key, schema validation) can be diagnosed instantly from error strings without spending tokens on a Haiku call. Only genuinely unrecognized failures need Claude. Keeps the feedback loop fast.

decisions_made.decision: Large prompt threshold raised from 1500 to 3000 chars
decisions_made.rationale: 1500 was too conservative. The real protection is multi-pass execution, not the gate. 3000 matches the planner truncation threshold and only catches prompts that will genuinely generate >6 files.

decisions_made.decision: Sub-agents are optional Chat Agent tools, not mandatory pipeline stages
decisions_made.rationale: Mandatory pipelines are hard to maintain and apply overhead to every build. Optional tools invoked by the Chat Agent only when needed give the same capability with zero overhead on simple builds.

decisions_made.decision: All sub-agents use Haiku at 0.1 temperature
decisions_made.rationale: Sub-agents are analytical tasks — low temperature improves precision. Haiku is fast and cheap enough to invoke mid-conversation without meaningful latency impact.

preferences.value: Never use 'refactor' — the correct term is 'reforge'
CommonJS (require/module.exports) for server — ES modules allowed in Vite/React frontend (Mode B only)
Plain HTML/CSS/JS (Mode A) — Vite+React (Mode B) only when explicitly requested
claude-opus-4-5 for all primary builds and reasoning tasks
Conversational — expect iteration, build on what works, don't rebuild from scratch
Direct, no hedging, no try-catch suggestions, no logging suggestions
Docx files for reference documents, .md for prompts and integration guides
Use 'I'll make this update' for changes, improvements, polish, and feature additions. Reserve 'I found the bug' for actual crashes, broken behavior, or runtime errors only.
Safe failure always preferred over destructive success — a build that does nothing is better than one that damages files

patterns_that_worked: Single Claude call with full platform constraints replaces multi-agent pipeline — simpler, fewer failure points
Memory extraction as a non-blocking post-build call — never blocks user-facing response
buildContext() prepended to system prompt as a separate block — clean separation of memory from instructions
Per-project conversation history stored in DB and passed as messages array — gives Claude full build journey awareness
Pre-emit self-checklist in builder prompt — model gates itself before output
Fence-strip + first-brace-scan pattern for all Claude JSON responses — handles all three Claude output styles
Preview proxy /api/assets/* passthrough — short-circuit to ForgeOS root handler, never forward to workspace app
Mode A / Mode B explicit runtime modes — prevents builder from guessing when to use Vite
Full syntax check (node --check) across all server JS files before every push
Three-phase visual editor — each phase useful standalone, phases compose into a full inspect-to-edit workflow
Dormant inspector script injected into all preview HTML — zero latency activation via postMessage
forgeos:jump-to-file custom event decouples RenderTab from EditTab — no prop threading needed
Plan gate constraint block prepended to builder system prompt — gives builder explicit permission and denial lists before it touches any file
suggestionToConstraintBlock safe fallback — locks all files when target cannot be parsed, never allows unconstrained build
isSuggestion flag threaded end-to-end (client → request body → createRun → run object → buildWorkspace) — clean signal for surgical build path with no ambiguity
Iteration rollback via GitHub API force-push — fast, no new commits, clean branch history
HubSpot skill seeded automatically on startup when token is present — zero manual setup for builders using /hubspot
PLAN: signal as Chat Agent escalation path — matches fix complexity to the right build mechanism without forcing the user to re-prompt
Planner prompt handling two input types — feature requests and Chat Agent diagnoses treated differently; diagnoses trusted as precise specifications with no extra scope added
Project history summarization via COALESCE concat — appends new summaries to existing history without overwriting; single column, no separate table needed
Tracker injection in pushToAppBranch file loop — intercepts all .html files at the moment of GitHub push, zero additional pipeline steps
sendBeacon + Blob fallback pattern for analytics — fires on page unload reliably across browsers without blocking navigation
buildTrackerScript(projectId, forgeOrigin) returning a complete script tag string — clean separation between tracker logic and injection point
Minimal consent banner + /privacy page pattern for public analytics — non-intrusive bottom banner, single OK button, honest privacy policy; legally sound for EU/CCPA without scary cookie walls
Two-layer large prompt guard (server 400 + client auto-route) — belt and suspenders; neither layer alone is sufficient
skipBuild: true on POST /api/projects — clean separation between project creation and build initiation; enables plan-first flow for new projects
AI Receptionist per-call Map state keyed by CallSid — lightweight, no DB needed for conversation state, auto-cleans on /call-complete
Multi-pass file accumulation — each pass receives all previously built files as existingFiles; later passes can correctly reference earlier ones
passProgress on run.stages.builder — surfaces pass-level progress to Plan tab via existing run polling without any new API surface or schema changes
Forge FORGE: signal for architecture diagnosis — correctly identified the three files, the root cause, and the fix structure before implementation
postBuildAnalysis as a non-blocking fire-and-forget hook — wired at all three failure paths, never delays pipeline, user sees diagnosis before they even open chat
KNOWN_FAILURE_PATTERNS as a lookup table — instant classification of common failures, extensible as new patterns are discovered, no Claude tokens spent on known issues
Optional tool pattern for sub-agents — Chat Agent invokes only when needed, zero overhead on simple builds, no maintenance coupling between agents
Single server.js with server-side HTML templates for kiosk/activation apps — zero build complexity, all future iterations are single-file Forge BUILD: suggestions
Testing routes directly against the Render URL (bypassing proxy) to isolate whether bugs are in the app or the proxy — saved significant debugging time on Canvas
curl -v tests against both direct Render URL and proxy URL to pinpoint exactly where responses diverge
mistakes_to_avoid: Inline backtick-wrapped content inside JS template literal strings — Node parser treats backtick as closing delimiter
Merging Claude Code PRs (claude/* branches) without review — PR #33 introduced syntax errors in builder.js and agents.js
Chat Agent suggesting logging or try-catch after failed iterations — named as degradation pattern, explicitly banned
Generated apps fetching assets server-side via localhost — always fetch client-side using root-relative /api/assets/FILENAME
getAssetsContext() returning array — must return formatted string for filter(Boolean).join() to work in builder.js
Fetch URL audit rule inverted — correct rule is: fetch argument must start with / and must not contain ://
Spreading process.env wholesale into child processes — host secrets bleed into published apps
URL helper functions (apiUrl(), buildUrl()) that construct absolute URLs — fix fetch() calls directly with /path strings
Multiple code changes in a single buildSuggestion — one root cause, one code change
Format rules at top of long prompts — model drops them under context pressure
Using execSync with shell string interpolation for paths — use execFileSync with args array

Running the plan gate on Chat Agent build suggestions — planner treats the suggestion as a feature request and generates a broad multi-file plan, which then gives the builder permission to touch files it should never touch
Force-push rollback without triggering Render redeploy — Render webhook may not fire on force-push since no new commit SHA is created; always verify the live site reflects the rollback

Trusting Forge's self-diagnosis on surgical build failures — Forge misidentified the root cause as 'executor not creating server.js' and proposed regenerating server.js on every HTML edit; real issue was createWorkspace() wiping the workspace; always trace the actual code path
Surgical builds writing only builder output without seeding existingFiles — createWorkspace wipes the dir, builder returns only the target file, all other files deleted
Using stopAllApps() in restoreIteration — kills every running workspace across all projects; always use stopApp(currentRunId) for project-scoped operations
Skipping loadAndRegisterRun after restoreIteration — workspace runs but UI cannot see it; runner Map must be updated after any workspace recreation

Attempting to restore a run with no snapshot — restoreIteration will fail; verify snapshot exists at stages.builder.output.files in run_snapshots before expecting restore to work; delete no-snapshot runs with deleteIteration instead
Exposing HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN to the frontend — all HubSpot calls must be server-side only; token must never appear in client-side code or API responses
Using PLAN: for single-line fixes — escalation to avoid writing a precise BUILD: line; always use BUILD: when the fix is one file, one code change

Forcing surgical BUILD: path for API migrations — when an external API changes endpoint or payload format, multiple routes must be rewritten together; surgical single-file path leaves the app in a broken partial state
Injecting analytics tracker into preview HTML — preview sessions are dev noise; published-only keeps analytics signal clean
Using recordMistake() directly for build failures — extractFailureMemory() produces structured lessons with categories; always use the structured path
Writing _fvid to localStorage before consent on public-facing sites — this is the specific ePrivacy trigger; always gate that write behind _fa_consent check on any site that may have EU visitors
Using Polly.Joanna voice in Twilio TwiML without verifying Polly add-on is activated — use alice for guaranteed availability on all accounts
Setting statusCallback inside a Twilio <Gather> verb — it is silently ignored; must be set at the Twilio console number level or via Twilio API
Bypassing the plan gate for large builds — prompts >1500 chars risk context overflow; always route through /plan first and build with approvedPlan
Patching ForgeOS infrastructure manually instead of letting Forge self-repair via FORGE: signal — undermines autonomous repair capability
Attempting to build >6 files in a single Claude call — context overflow is guaranteed; always use multi-pass for large builds
Asking Forge to implement multi-pass pipeline using the broken single-pass pipeline — circular dependency; infrastructure changes to the build pipeline must be made directly
Passing full original prompt to every multi-pass build pass — causes context overflow on pass 1; each pass must receive a scoped prompt derived from pass.description and a brief prompt excerpt only
Sending all accumulated files to every pass in buildWorkspaceMultiPass — context grows unboundedly; filter to only files relevant to each pass
Clearing pendingForgePlan before the apply call completes — keeps the user blind during a 30-60s operation; always show forgeApplying loading state first

Large prompt threshold too low — 1500 chars catches legitimate prompts and wastes plan gate cycles; threshold should be 3000 to match planner truncation and target only genuinely large multi-file builds
Chat Agent asking for information already in context — diagnostics, iteration history, source files, and runtime logs are always injected; any question about these is a banned behavior
Using NEON_DATABASE_URL for per-app isolated databases in published services — it is reserved and cannot be overridden. Use APP_DATABASE_URL or a descriptive custom name.
Proxy fetch without redirect:'manual' — Node follows redirects silently, returns 200 with final destination HTML, breaks browser navigation entirely.
JSON.stringify(req.body) in proxy for form POST requests — must use new URLSearchParams(req.body).toString() for application/x-www-form-urlencoded and delete the original content-length header.
Applying cookie-session as global middleware — scope it to the routes that need it to avoid session middleware errors breaking public routes.

Standard HTML form POSTs in published apps going through the ForgeOS proxy — use fetch() with Content-Type application/json instead. The proxy handles JSON correctly from day one; urlencoded form encoding through the proxy is unreliable.

Cookie-session for admin auth in published apps through ForgeOS proxy — Set-Cookie headers are unreliable through the proxy. Use bearer tokens stored in localStorage with token passed as query param on initial authenticated page loads.

key_terminology.value: The correct term for what other systems call 'refactor' — we reforge code, never refactor it
The AI-powered web app builder cockpit, deployed on Render at forge-os.ai
A directory containing files for a single generated app, identified by runId
The persistent memory module (server/memory/brain.js) storing patterns, mistakes, preferences and project history across all ForgeOS builds
The ForgeOS UI — tab strip (Plan/Review/Diff/Auditor/Render/Edit/Shell/DB/Env/Publish) plus global Assets sidebar and collapsible chat column
Plain Node runtime — node server.js, CommonJS, no bundlers, plain HTML/CSS/JS frontend. Default mode.
Vite+React runtime — npm run dev, Vite bundler, React or Vue frontend. Opt-in only.
Files uploaded to forge_assets table in Neon, accessible at /api/assets/:filename by any project
The ForgeOS middleware that serves apps at /preview/:runId/ and rewrites HTML URLs to stay within the proxy path
A structured JSON export of project knowledge in a model-agnostic format for portability between AI systems
The Chat Agent failure mode where after failed iterations it suggests logging or try-catch instead of finding the actual broken code
The ForgeOS settings feature that auto-injects API keys and secrets as environment variables at runtime
Three-phase inspect-to-edit system: Phase 1 (Inspect Mode), Phase 2 (Edit tab), Phase 3 (Inspect-to-Edit bridge)
Custom window event carrying {file, line} — dispatched by RenderTab after a successful file search, consumed by EditTab to jump to the matched location
The pre-build approval step where generatePlan() produces a structured plan for user review before /iterate fires. Only triggers for raw chat prompts, not suggestion builds.

A build triggered by a Chat Agent buildSuggestion. Bypasses the plan gate. Uses suggestionToConstraintBlock to lock all files except the parsed target. Identified by isSuggestion: true on the run object.

Function in server/plan/manager.js that parses the target file from a Chat Agent suggestion using regex, builds a single-file constraint block, and returns { constraintBlock, targetFile }. Safe fallback locks all files if regex fails.

Function in server/projects/manager.js and endpoint DELETE /api/projects/:id/runs/:runId that removes an iteration from DB, deletes the run snapshot, and auto-rolls currentRunId back to the previous iteration.

Function in server/projects/manager.js and endpoint POST /api/projects/:id/restore/:runId. Loads snapshot from Neon, stops current project app, recreates workspace, installs deps, starts app, calls loadAndRegisterRun, updates project.currentRunId. Fails fast with error if snapshot missing.

Function in server/pipeline/runner.js. Loads a past run snapshot from Neon and registers it in the in-memory runs Map. Required after restoreIteration so the UI can poll the restored run's status and files.

server/integrations/hubspot.js — HubSpot REST API wrapper. Methods: searchContacts, createContact, updateContact, searchDeals, createDeal, getStatus. Uses HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN from Global Secrets Vault. API routes at /api/hubspot/*. Chat Agent has hubspot_query tool. HubSpot CRM skill auto-seeded on startup when token is present. Activated in builds with /hubspot in chat.

Chat Agent output prefix for multi-step coordinated fixes. Triggers the full plan gate instead of surgical build. Used for: deprecated API migrations, multi-route rewrites, package.json + code changes, SDK replacements. Counterpart to BUILD: (surgical single-file). planSuggestion is exempt from banned-pattern validation. Purple 'Generate Plan' button appears in chat UI.

Chat Agent chooses based on fix scope: BUILD: = one root cause, one file, one code change (surgical); PLAN: = multiple coordinated changes required. Inspect-mode always produces BUILD:. Never use PLAN: for single-line fixes. Never use BUILD: for API migrations that require touching multiple routes.

TEXT column on the projects table. Accumulated summaries of conversation turns that have rolled out of the 10-turn window. Prepended to builder context as PROJECT HISTORY block. Updated via COALESCE concat in summarizeAndTrimConversation().

Function in server/memory/brain.js. Haiku call that produces { lesson, category } JSON from a failed build. Called at all three failure stages (build, install-failed, start-failed). Replaces raw recordMistake() at those paths.

Neon table storing all analytics events from published apps. Columns: project_id, session_id, visitor_id, event_type, url, referrer, properties JSONB, created_at BIGINT. Indexed for fast per-project range queries.
Function in server/analytics/tracker.js. Returns a self-contained IIFE <script> tag with projectId and forgeOrigin baked in. Injected before </body> in all .html files during pushToAppBranch.
Public analytics API injected into every published app. Usage: window.forge_track('event_name', { ...data }). Queues a custom event for the next beacon flush. No setup or import required.

Cockpit tab component with six views (Overview, Pages, Events, Performance, Errors, Devices) and a 1h/24h/7d/30d range selector. Fetches from /api/analytics/:projectId/* endpoints. Web Vitals rated good/needs-improvement/poor per Google thresholds.

Pattern for GDPR-compliant analytics on public-facing sites. _fvid localStorage write gated behind localStorage.getItem('_fa_consent') === '1'. Session/vitals/errors fire freely. Consent set via bottom-of-page banner with OK button linking to /privacy page. Banner suppressed on /privacy route and when consent already set.

Two-layer enforcement preventing large prompts from bypassing the plan approval flow. Server: POST /iterate returns 400 { requiresPlan: true } when prompt > 1500 chars without approvedPlan. Client: startProject auto-creates project with skipBuild: true then routes through /plan. Both bypassed by approvedPlan or isSuggestion.

Parameter on POST /api/projects. When true, creates the project record in DB without firing the builder. Returns { id, runId: null }. Used by the large prompt plan gate so the project exists before the plan is generated and approved.

Builder failure mode where total Claude input tokens (system prompt + schema + conversation + prompt) exceed capacity, causing truncated JSON output that fails to parse. Identified by position >10000 in JSON parse errors. Root cause: model-router.js schema in system prompt or builder.js uncapped memory/assets context. Chat Agent should fire FORGE: on this pattern.

Twilio Voice + Claude + Resend + HubSpot pipeline at sandbox-ai-receptionist.forge-os.ai. Handles callback/message, scheduling, and services inquiries. Per-call state in Map keyed by CallSid. Voice: alice. statusCallback configured in Twilio console Call status changes field.

Build mode triggered when planner detects >6 total files. Outputs multiPass: true with passes array (3-6 files each). buildWorkspaceMultiPass executes passes sequentially, accumulating files across passes. Plan tab shows 'Pass N of M' progress. Approve once, all passes execute autonomously.

Function in server/builder.js. Takes prompt, existingFiles, passes array, projectId, approvedPlan, run. Iterates passes, calls buildWorkspace per pass with scoped constraint block, merges output. Updates run.stages.builder.passProgress for Plan tab visibility.

Optional second parameter to planToConstraintBlock(). When provided, generates a pass-scoped constraint block listing only that pass's files. Used by buildWorkspaceMultiPass to constrain each individual builder call.

Function in server/chat/manager.js. Fires automatically after every failed build via hook in builder.js. Classifies failure using KNOWN_FAILURE_PATTERNS (no Claude call for known patterns), saves diagnosis as assistant message. User sees root cause before asking.

Lookup table in server/chat/manager.js. 6 patterns mapping error signatures to instant diagnoses: context_overflow → FORGE:, missing_api_key → plain, json_schema_validation → FORGE:, start_failed → BUILD:, install_failed → plain, build_failed_generic → plain. Extend this table as new failure modes are discovered.

Chat Agent behavioral mandate in CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS. First response on a failed build must be a diagnosis. Never ask for information already in context. [AUTO-ANALYSIS] prefixed messages are automated diagnoses — build on them, do not repeat.

A focused Claude Haiku API call invoked by the Chat Agent via the invoke_agent tool. Four types: file_analyst, failure_analyst, fix_verifier, spec_analyst. Optional — only invoked when the task exceeds single-context analysis. Never mandatory pipeline stages.

Sub-agent in server/agents/manager.js. Takes a complex build prompt, returns structured JSON: features, buildOrder (dependency-ordered), fileGroups (3-5 files each), riskAreas, estimatedPasses. Use before the planner on Canvas-scale builds.

Sub-agent in server/agents/manager.js. Takes existing files + proposed fix, checks imports, interface matching, syntax, side effects. Returns VALID/ISSUES/VERDICT. Use for multi-file fixes before spending a build cycle.
